Fern PoLITICAL PnaCTICES CorraurrsSIoN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95514-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
April4,2003
Lieutenant Jack J. Anderson
County of Orange
Sheriffs Department
Professional Standards Division
550 North Flower Street
Santa Ana, CA 92703
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-033
Dear Lt. Anderson:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the Orange County
Sheriff.s Department regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (ihe
'Act').1
QUESTTON
1. Can the sheriffs signature, name, and title be included in a signature block below a message
that explains the purpose of a community feedback questionnaire?
2. If each questionnaire were personalized by adding the name and address of the recipient, the
date the recipient requested police services, and the reason for the request, would the
questionnaires be excluded from the mass mailing prohibition?
CONCLUSION
l. No. Inclusion of the sheriff s name and signature (as described, and as indicated on the draft
questionnaire you provided) is prohibited.
2. No. Two items are substantially similar when most of the issues of public concern mentioned
in one item are mentioned in the otheq and most of the information contained in one item is
contained in the other. The proposed questionnaires would differ only in the name, address,
service requested by the intended recipient, and date of request.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
!:=:


FileNo. 4-03-033
Page No. 2
FACTS
The Orange County SherifPs Deparfinent, in the course of routine business, is preparing
to mail out a community feedback questionnaire for the purpose of improving public ruf"iy
services. The questionnaire would be sent to a random sampling of either tOX of thor" *ho
called forpolice services within the sherifPs jurisdiction or some percentage that would yield
fewer thaa200 mailings per month. The questionnaire is intended to allow the sheriff to analyze
the level of service being provided by his employees.
ANALYSIS
Section 89001 provides that "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expe,nse." Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) clarifies that amailing is prohibited only if ali of
the following apply:
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her residence,
place of employment orbusiness, orpost officebox. Forpurposes ofthis subdivision
(aXl), the item delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
"(Z)The item sent either:
"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends
the mailing, or
"(B) Includes the name, ofEce, photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.
"(3) (A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys; or
"@) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 area paid with
public moneys, and the design, production, orprinting is done with the intent of
sending the item other than as permifted by this regulation.
"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single calendar
month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited requesiand any item
described in subdivision (b) fexceptions]." @egulation 1g901(a).)
signafure Block: rnclusion of a Reference to an Elected officer
A mass mailing which does not include the name, photograph or any reference to an
elected officer who is affiliated with the agency that produces or distributes the mailing is not
subject to the restrictions of the regulation. (Regulation 1S901(a)(2).) An elected officer is
"affiliated" with an agency if the officer 'ls a member, officer or employee of the agerLcy, or a


File No. A-03-033
Page No. 3
subunit thereof such as a committee" of that agency;has supervisory control over the agency; or
appoints any of the mernbers of the agetcy. (Regulation 1S901(c)(l).) The sheriff is an elected
official affiliated with the agency producing the questionnaire.
If the mailing has been prepared or sent in "cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concetrt" with the elected officer, any use of the elected officer's name, photograph or office, or
any reference to the officer is prohibited. (Regulation 18901(a)(2XB).)
The proposed signature block includes the name, office, and signature of the sheriff In
addition, the message regarding the purpose of the questionnaire contains a reference to the
office of sheriff, The signature of the sheriffand the personal message indicate that the mailing
was prepared or sent in "cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert" with the sheriff.
Under these circumstances, any use of the sherifPs name, photograph or office is prohibited.
Substantially Similar
Regulation 18901 provides that two items are "substantially similar,, if,
"3. Both of the following apply to the itEms mailed:
"a. Most of the bills, legislation, governmental action, activities, events, or issues of
public concern mentioned in one item are mentioned in the other.
"b. Most of the information contained in one item is contained in the other." (Regulation
18e01(c)(3XAX3).)
With regard to the proposed questionnaire, both these criteria are met and the items
would therefore be considered "substantially similar.',
Exceptions to Prohibition
Unsolicited Request
Regulation 1890I(a)(a) excludes from the prohibition against mass mailings any item
sent in response to an unsolicited request. The proposed questionnaire does not fitlnto ihis
exception, because you indicated that your source of intended recipients for the proposed
questionnaire was former requestors of police services.
Essential to Program Function
Regulation 18901 does not prohibit the mass mailing of:
"[a]ny item sent by an agency responsible for administering a govemment program, to
persons subject to that program, in any instance where the mailing of such item is
essential to the functioning of the progftrm, where the item does not include the elected
officer's photograph; and where use of the elected officer's name, office, title, or signafure
is necessary to the functioning of the program." (Regulation 1s901(bx6).)


File No. 4-03-033
Page No. 4
The proposed questionnaire does not fit into this exception, because the use of the
sherifPs nzlme, office, title, and signature on this questionnaire is not necessary to the
functioning of a program administered by the sherifPs office.
Normal Course of Business
Communications sent in the normal course of business are not prohibited if they are sent
to governmental entities or officers, or sent intra-agency.to employees, deputies, or other staff.
(Regulation 18901(b)(3X4).) This communication does not fit this exception because recipients
will be non-govenrmental entities outside the sheriffs office.
200 Pieces In Calendar Month
Please note that the statute and regulation prohibiting mass mailings at public expe,lrse
apply only to mailings of more than 200 substantially similar pieces in a calendar month. If
fewer than20l questionnaires were mailed in a calendar month, the mailing would not be
prohibited by the Political Reform Act.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Hal Dasinger
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosure
HDje
